description_long: This hammer features a large, irregularly shaped stone head secured to a smooth, light brown wooden handle using crisscrossed beige cord or sinew. The stone head is roughly oval with visible chips and facets, giving it a primitive, hand-crafted appearance. The handle is straight, slightly tapered, and appears to be sanded smooth, with no visible grip enhancements. The binding is prominent, tightly wrapping the head to the handle, and is visible from all angles. The hammer lacks any modern features such as a claw or metal components, emphasizing a simple, utilitarian design. There are no visible brand markings, and the object appears clean and unused.
description: A primitive hammer with a large, chipped stone head bound to a smooth wooden handle by beige cord, showing a hand-crafted, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The broad face of the stone head and the full length of the wooden handle are visible, with the binding clearly wrapping the head.
description_view_1: A top-down view showing the narrow edge of the stone head and the alignment of the handle, with the binding visible along the sides.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the hammer, displaying the other face of the stone head and the handle, with the binding pattern mirrored.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view highlighting the underside of the stone head and the end of the handle, with the binding visible at the junction.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, smooth, light brown, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: large, irregular stone head, roughly oval, no claw or specialized striking faces, primitive sledge type
